dialogos.texto_en: Ah, big bro!
Hmm?\nWhat are the two of you doing?
Just taking a break.\nWe're thirsty.
I see, take your time and rest.\nI'll go check out some other things,\nso don't be late for our meeting time.
Don't go.
Uwaaa?!\nWhat's up, all of a sudden?
Futaro, why don't you take a break too?\nYou must be tired from walking around.
*sigh*\nWell, I am a little tired.
Okay, then it's settled.
Alright.
That's right, look, big bro.\nSee, there's a tea that's only available in Okinawa.\nIt's called sanpin-cha.
Wow, for sure, I've never seen it before.
It's different from green tea and black tea.\nIt has a unique taste, but it's delicious.
Oh, I'm curious about the taste.\nCan I have a sip?
Oh, sorry. I already finished mine.
F-Futarooo?
What's up?
Um, well... I have half of mine left...
A-actually, nevermind!
Huh?
Th-they sell it at that shop over there.\nWhy don't you buy some, Futaro?
Ah, get me one too!\nI want one more.
Sure, wait a minute.
Oh, this is really refreshing.\nAnd there's a faint sweet fragrance too.
Yeah, it's delicious.\nI think it goes well with sweet snacks.
I think this is like jasmine tea.
Yeah, it's a type of jasmine tea.\nSanpin-cha is actually a word in the Okinawan language.
Jasmine tea is a type of Chinese tea, right?\nYou seem to know more about tea than I do, Miku.
Huh? Me?
Well, I think I read somewhere...
That, the difference between green tea and black tea\nis the degree of fermentation.\nIs jasmine tea the same?
If you don't know, Miku, then we're stuck.
Wait, let me check on my phone.
Um...
Jasmine tea is green tea scented with jasmine.
You stack green tea leaves and jasmine flowers\nto transfer the fragrance of the flowers\nto the tea leaves.
So, this is also green tea.
That's pretty complicated.\nTea types can be quite complex.
That's true, but that's what makes it interesting.
Big bro, you like barley tea,\nand it's actually made from barley, right?
Yeah, they boil barley to make it.
Oh, then is genmaicha made from rice?
Let me see...\nit says here that genmaicha is made\nby mixing green tea and roasted brown rice.
Green tea again here, too.\nIt seems it makes a big difference\ndepending on what you mix it with.
Does adding a fragrance have an effect?
I feel like Nino said that the scent of jasmine\nhas a refreshing effect.
Maybe this tea is the same.
Well, I do feel refreshed, that's for sure.
A scent's effect is important, isn't it?
Come to think of it, on my birthday,\nNino gave me something with a scent that's\nsupposed to have a fatigue-reducing effect.
What was it again... Aloha?
Big bro, that's clothing.
Teehee. If it's an Aloha shirt you want,\nthey might sell it in Okinawa too.
Oh, then is genmaicha made from rice?
Let me see...\nit says here that genmaicha is made\nby mixing green tea and roasted brown rice.
Green tea again here, too.\nIt seems it makes a big difference\ndepending on what you mix it with.
I'm impressed by the potential of green tea.
Yeah. Just by changing the processing method,\nit can change so much.
And the wisdom of the people from the past\nwho brought out its potential is also amazing.
Maybe we should try drinking more of other types of tea.\nWe might find a new favorite.
But I still think green tea is the best.
It seems sanpin-cha was pretty good.\nBut, it just can't beat green tea, huh?
Aren't you starting to crave matcha soda now?
Haha, we've only been in Okinawa for three days,\nyou know.
Oh, then is genmaicha made from rice?
Let me see...\nit says here that genmaicha is made\nby mixing green tea and roasted brown rice.
Green tea again here, too.\nIt seems it makes a big difference\ndepending on what you mix it with.
People are the same way. They can change drastically\ndepending on who they're with.
If you're with someone for a long time,\ntheir influence also becomes significant.\nYou know that too, right, Futaro?
Well, I understand what\nyou're trying to say...
You changed me into who I am, Futaro.
Hey...\nDon't say that in a way that could be misunderstood.
Teehee, but it's true.
(Since we left the hotel, something's been off.\n What's going on?)
